status_messages.what_was_done: Collected intent: target='customers', purpose='cluster customers based on their shopping behaviour and pref'
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that the dataset file actually exists.

user_intent.business_purpose: cluster customers based on their shopping behaviour and preferences

status_messages.what_was_done: Profiled 50,000×23 dataset. Found 11 numeric cols. Claude suggested 7 feature groups.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not sample sub-populations or run hypothesis tests for distribution differences.
status_messages.doubts: Suggested groups are based on column names; actual feature buildability depends on data quality. Mean skewness=3.6.
issues: Mean feature skewness=3.6 is high. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

transaction_spend_profile.description: Captures how much customers spend per transaction, including average, variance, and distribution of spend amounts to distinguish high-value vs. budget shoppers.
transaction_spend_profile.rationale: Spend amount is the most direct indicator of shopping behaviour and customer value tier. Aggregating per customer reveals consistent big spenders, frugal buyers, and erratic spenders.

transaction_frequency_recency.description: Captures how often a customer transacts and how recently they were active, enabling RFM-style segmentation (Recency, Frequency).
transaction_frequency_recency.rationale: Frequency and recency are cornerstones of behavioural segmentation — they distinguish loyal frequent shoppers from one-time or lapsed customers, directly supporting the business purpose.

category_preference.description: Captures which merchant categories a customer gravitates toward (e.g., health & fitness, shopping, misc), encoding their lifestyle and purchase intent as category-level proportions or dominant category.
category_preference.rationale: Category mix reveals distinct shopper personas (e.g., health-conscious, impulse buyers, online-first) and is a rich behavioural signal that goes beyond raw spend.

geographic_mobility.description: Captures the spatial spread between a customer's home location and the merchants they transact with, reflecting whether they shop locally or across a wide geographic range.
geographic_mobility.rationale: Distance between customer and merchant location can distinguish urban convenience shoppers from travelers or online-heavy customers, and city population adds urban vs. rural context.

demographic_profile.description: Captures static customer attributes including gender, age derived from date of birth, and home city population size as a proxy for urban/rural lifestyle.
demographic_profile.rationale: Demographics provide stable segmentation axes that, when combined with behavioural features, help distinguish whether spend patterns correlate with life stage or geography.

temporal_behavior.description: Captures when customers tend to shop — time of day, day of week, and weekend vs. weekday patterns — to identify night-owl shoppers, weekend splurgers, or regular weekday buyers.
temporal_behavior.rationale: Shopping timing is a strong behavioural differentiator; customers who consistently shop late at night or on weekends represent meaningfully different lifestyle segments from 9-to-5 lunchtime shoppers.

fraud_risk_signal.description: Captures each customer's historical fraud rate as a behavioural risk dimension, distinguishing accounts with elevated fraud incidence from clean transaction histories.
fraud_risk_signal.rationale: While not a traditional shopping behaviour, a customer-level fraud rate is a meaningful signal for risk-based segmentation and may co-cluster with unusual spend or geographic patterns worth flagging.

status_messages.what_was_done: Engineered 263 features for 983 customers from 1,296,675 transactions. Builders used: ['category_behavior', 'category_trend', 'category_loyalty', 'overall_spend', 'overall_frequency', 'merchant_diversity', 'geographic_mobility', 'temporal_patterns', 'demographic'].
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not engineer features requiring external data (e.g. credit scores, product catalogue).
status_messages.doubts: Some features may have many zeros for customers with short history. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 263 features (PCA+AE). Removed 156 via VIF gate. Claude selected 39 from 107 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 3 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Used KMeans with k=7 (auto-selected via silhouette). Ran deepening loop → 7 leaf clusters. Silhouette=0.1541 (weak but usable).
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not try DBSCAN or GMM (not in current skill set).
status_messages.doubts: Silhouette improved 0.107→0.154 across k range — marginal gain.
issues: Silhouette=0.154 < 0.25 — clusters may overlap slightly. Consider different k or algorithm.

status_messages.what_was_done: Named 7 clusters using Claude (tone='easy'). Clarity Gate FAILED. Avg confidence=3.3/10.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that description text references specific numbers (only checked confidence and name uniqueness).
status_messages.doubts: Low-confidence clusters: C0(conf=3), C1(conf=3), C2(conf=4), C3(conf=3), C4(conf=4), C5(conf=3), C6(conf=3)
issues: Avg LLM confidence 3.3 < 6.0

status_messages.what_was_done: Trained RF, 5-fold stratified CV. CV macro-F1=0.971 ≥ 0.7 threshold.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not compute SHAP values.
status_messages.doubts: Lowest-F1 personas: The Frequent-but-Not-Daily Card User(0.92), The Regular Daily Payer (Slightly Less Active)(0.95), The High-Frequency Everyday Swiper(0.96)

llm_queries.purpose: suggest feature engineering groups from schema + business purpose
design feature engineering plan (behavior × topic × time_window)
select best feature subset from 107 VIF-filtered candidates
